# Frontend Performance Deep Dive (Senior React Interview Guide)

These topics are heavily asked in **Senior React / Frontend Engineer** interviews.

***

# 1. Event Loop, Microtasks vs Macrotasks

JavaScript is **single-threaded**, meaning it executes one task at a time using:

```text
Call Stack
↓
Microtask Queue
↓
Macrotask Queue
↓
Event Loop
```

The event loop continuously checks whether the call stack is empty and then processes queued work. Microtasks have higher priority than macrotasks. [\[dev.to\]](https://dev.to/satishjaiswal/javascript-event-loop-explained-macrotasks-microtasks-and-asyncawait-made-simple-22cd), [\[developer....ozilla.org\]](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/API/HTML_DOM_API/Microtask_guide), [\[tutorialre...erence.com\]](https://tutorialreference.com/javascript/browser-and-events/miscellaneous-browser/javascript-event-loop-microtasks-macrotasks)

***

## Execution Flow

```text
Sync Code
↓
Microtasks
↓
Rendering
↓
Macrotasks
```

After a task finishes, the browser drains the microtask queue before moving to the next macrotask. [\[developer....ozilla.org\]](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/API/HTML_DOM_API/Microtask_guide), [\[tutorialre...erence.com\]](https://tutorialreference.com/javascript/browser-and-events/miscellaneous-browser/javascript-event-loop-microtasks-macrotasks)

***

## Microtasks

Examples:

```js
Promise.then()
catch()
finally()

queueMicrotask()

async/await continuation
```

Promise callbacks, `queueMicrotask`, and code after `await` run in the microtask queue. [\[dev.to\]](https://dev.to/satishjaiswal/javascript-event-loop-explained-macrotasks-microtasks-and-asyncawait-made-simple-22cd), [\[developer....ozilla.org\]](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/API/HTML_DOM_API/Microtask_guide)

***

## Macrotasks

Examples:

```js
setTimeout()

setInterval()

DOM Events

Network callbacks
```

These are scheduled in the task (macrotask) queue. [\[dev.to\]](https://dev.to/satishjaiswal/javascript-event-loop-explained-macrotasks-microtasks-and-asyncawait-made-simple-22cd), [\[developer....ozilla.org\]](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/API/HTML_DOM_API/Microtask_guide)

***

## Interview Example

```js
console.log("1");

setTimeout(() => {
  console.log("2");
}, 0);

Promise.resolve().then(() => {
  console.log("3");
});

console.log("4");
```

Output:

```text
1
4
3
2
```

Why?

```text
Sync Code:
1
4

Microtasks:
3

Macrotasks:
2
```

Microtasks run before macrotasks. [\[dev.to\]](https://dev.to/antonov_mike/event-loop-call-stack-web-api-task-queue-microtask-queue-1eb2), [\[dev.to\]](https://dev.to/satishjaiswal/javascript-event-loop-explained-macrotasks-microtasks-and-asyncawait-made-simple-22cd)

***

# 2. Critical Rendering Path (CRP)

CRP is the sequence browsers follow to display a page.

```text
HTML
 ↓
DOM
 ↓
CSS
 ↓
CSSOM
 ↓
Render Tree
 ↓
Layout
 ↓
Paint
 ↓
Composite
```

***

## Visual Flow

```text
HTML
   ↓
 DOM
   ↓
CSSOM
   ↓
Render Tree
   ↓
 Layout
   ↓
 Paint
```

***

## Example

&lt;div&gt;Hello&lt;/div&gt;![Visualization](data:image/png;base64,)

Browser:

### Step 1

Parse HTML

```text
DOM
```

### Step 2

Parse CSS

```text
CSSOM
```

### Step 3

Create Render Tree

```text
DOM + CSSOM
```

### Step 4

Layout

```text
Width
Height
Position
```

### Step 5

Paint

```text
Background
Text
Borders
```

***

## CRP Optimisation

### Minify CSS

```text
Less parsing
```

### Inline Critical CSS

&lt;style&gt;.hero{ color:red}&lt;/style&gt;![Visualization](data:image/png;base64,)

### Defer JS

app.jsscript&gt;![Visualization](data:image/png;base64,)

### Optimise Images

```text
WebP
AVIF
Lazy Loading
```

***

# 3. Bundle Optimisation, Code Splitting & Lazy Loading

***

## Problem

Single bundle:

```text
bundle.js

3 MB
```

User downloads everything.

```text
Dashboard
Admin
Reports
Settings
```

Even if not used.

***

## Code Splitting

Split code into smaller chunks.

```text
main.js

dashboard.js

admin.js

settings.js
```

***

## React.lazy()

```jsx
import React, {
  Suspense,
  lazy
} from "react";

const Dashboard =
  lazy(() =>
    import("./Dashboard")
  );

export default function App() {
  return (
    <Suspense
      fallback={<div>Loading...</div>}
    >
      <Dashboard />
    </Suspense>
  );
}
```

Bundle loads only when required.

***

## Route Based Splitting

```jsx
const Home =
  lazy(() =>
    import("./pages/Home")
  );

const Products =
  lazy(() =>
    import("./pages/Products")
  );
```

***

## Dynamic Import

```js
const module =
  await import(
    "./utils"
  );
```

***

## Benefits

```text
✅ Smaller Initial Bundle

✅ Faster LCP

✅ Faster TTI

✅ Better Core Web Vitals
```

***

# 4. Reflow vs Repaint

***

## Reflow (Layout)

Reflow occurs when layout changes.

Examples:

```js
element.style.width =
  "400px";

element.style.height =
  "300px";
```

Browser recalculates:

```text
Position
Width
Height
Layout
```

***

## Repaint

Visual changes only.

```js
element.style.color =
 "red";
```

No layout changes.

Browser updates:

```text
Color
Border
Background
```

***

## Cost

```text
Reflow
  ↓
Expensive

Repaint
  ↓
Less Expensive
```

***

## Interview Example

### Causes Reflow

```js
element.style.width =
  "200px";
```

### Causes Repaint Only

```js
element.style.background =
  "red";
```

***

## Layout Thrashing

Bad:

```js
for(let i=0;i<1000;i++){

 element.style.width =
  i + "px";

 element.offsetWidth;
}
```

Every iteration:

```text
Write
Read
Write
Read
```

Forces repeated layouts.

***

## Better

```js
requestAnimationFrame(
 () => {
   element.style.width =
      "500px";
 }
);
```

***

# 5. HTTP/1.1 vs HTTP/2

***

# HTTP/1.1

Problem:

```text
One request per connection
```

Browser opens:

```text
6 connections
```

to same domain.

***

## Waterfall

```text
Request 1
wait

Request 2
wait

Request 3
wait
```

Head-of-line blocking becomes a bottleneck.

***

# HTTP/2

Introduces:

```text
Multiplexing
```

Single connection:

```text
Connection
 ├─ Req1
 ├─ Req2
 ├─ Req3
 ├─ Req4
 └─ Req5
```

Multiple requests can share the same connection concurrently.

***

## HTTP/1.1

```text
GET CSS
wait

GET JS
wait

GET Image
wait
```

***

## HTTP/2

```text
GET CSS
GET JS
GET Image

Parallel Streams
```

***

## Features of HTTP/2

### Multiplexing

Multiple requests on one connection.

### Header Compression

```text
Smaller headers
```

### Stream Prioritisation

```text
Important resources
first
```

### Server Push (historically supported)

```text
Send assets proactively
```

***

## Performance Impact

```text
HTTP/1.1

CSS
JS
Image
Fonts

Many connections
```

```text
HTTP/2

Single connection

Multiplexed streams
```

Better resource utilisation and reduced connection overhead.

***

# Senior Frontend Interview Summary

> The Event Loop coordinates synchronous execution, microtasks, and macrotasks, with microtasks always executing before macrotasks. The Critical Rendering Path describes how browsers convert HTML/CSS into pixels on the screen. Bundle optimisation improves performance through code splitting and lazy loading. Rendering performance depends on avoiding expensive reflows and unnecessary repaints. At the network layer, HTTP/2 significantly improves loading performance through multiplexing, header compression, and stream prioritisation compared with HTTP/1.1. [\[dev.to\]](https://dev.to/satishjaiswal/javascript-event-loop-explained-macrotasks-microtasks-and-asyncawait-made-simple-22cd), [\[developer....ozilla.org\]](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/API/HTML_DOM_API/Microtask_guide), [\[tutorialre...erence.com\]](https://tutorialreference.com/javascript/browser-and-events/miscellaneous-browser/javascript-event-loop-microtasks-macrotasks)

### Interview Tip

A common senior-level question is:

```js
console.log("start");

setTimeout(() => console.log("timeout"));

Promise.resolve().then(() =>
  console.log("promise")
);

console.log("end");
```

Output:

```text
start
end
promise
timeout
```

Explain it confidently using:

```text
Call Stack
Microtask Queue
Macrotask Queue
Event Loop
```

That's one of the most frequently asked frontend interview topics. [\[dev.to\]](https://dev.to/satishjaiswal/javascript-event-loop-explained-macrotasks-microtasks-and-asyncawait-made-simple-22cd), [\[dev.to\]](https://dev.to/antonov_mike/event-loop-call-stack-web-api-task-queue-microtask-queue-1eb2)
